translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timotheus the brother, to Philemon our beloved and fellow-worker,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Apphia the beloved, and Archippus our fellow-soldier, and the assembly in thy house:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Grace to you, and peace, from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I give thanks to my God, always making mention of thee in my prayers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: hearing of thy love and faith that thou hast unto the Lord Jesus and toward all the saints,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that the fellowship of thy faith may become working in the full knowledge of every good thing that [is] in you toward Christ Jesus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for we have much joy and comfort in thy love, because the bowels of the saints have been refreshed through thee, brother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore, having in Christ much boldness to command thee that which is fit --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because of the love I rather entreat, being such an one as Paul the aged, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I entreat thee concerning my child -- whom I did beget in my bonds -- Onesimus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who once was to thee unprofitable, and now is profitable to me and to thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whom I did send again, and thou him (that is, my own bowels) receive,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whom I did wish to retain to myself, that in thy behalf he might minister to me in the bonds of the good news,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and apart from thy mind I willed to do nothing, that as of necessity thy good deed may not be, but of willingness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for perhaps because of this he did depart for an hour, that age-duringly thou mayest have him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: no more as a servant, but above a servant -- a brother beloved, especially to me, and how much more to thee, both in the flesh and in the Lord!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If, then, with me thou hast fellowship, receive him as me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if he did hurt to thee, or doth owe anything, this to me be reckoning;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I, Paul did write with my hand, I -- I will repay; that I may not say that also thyself, besides, to me thou dost owe.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yes, brother, may I have profit of thee in the Lord; refresh my bowels in the Lord;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having been confident in thy obedience I did write to thee, having known that also above what I may say thou wilt do;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and at the same time also prepare for me a lodging, for I hope that through your prayers I shall be granted to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Salute thee doth Epaphras, (my fellow-captive in Christ Jesus,)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Markus, Aristarchus, Demas, Lukas, my fellow-workmen!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [is] with your spirit! Amen.
